And I was like, that dude made $80,000 in the last 90 minutes. The next speaker got up, he did this thing, he sold, and I saw that thing happen over and over and over again all weekend, and I was like, I gotta learn how to do this. And I assumed that because I had really good content, I'd be an amazing webinar presenter, and actually this is pre-webinar, but I thought it'd be good selling from stage, and so I invited to go to a seminar, I got on stage and I started speaking, I did my whole thing the way that I thought it would be the best, I delivered the best content I knew possible, and we launched it, and I finished, I did my clothes, and nobody, not one person, got up to go to the back of the room, and it was really awkward. It was one of those things where like, typically when you get out of speaking, someone turns the music on, no one turns the music on, nobody moved, it was awkward silence, and I awkwardly just walked off the stage, and I walked to the back of the room, a couple of people shook my hand, and then I went to my hotel room, and I literally locked myself in my hotel room for the next two days of the event, because I didn't want to see anybody, and I watched movies and ate Hagen-Dazs ice cream for two days, kind of embarrassing. But it was like the most humiliating thing in the world, and I was like, I will never try to sell from stage again, no matter what. But then a little while later, I was like, I saw people doing it, I'm like, I have to figure this out. So over the next 10 years of my life, over a decade, I think I've been to about 12 different public speaking trainings on how to sell from stage, and then I went out there and I did it hundreds, and hundreds, and hundreds of times, and based on that, we actually built a script that I call the perfect webinar. And this is it, if you've read the Doc-Om-Segers book, you've probably seen it before, but this is the script. When you have this script, you can sell pretty much anything, I've had people sell supplements with this, we've had people sell coach, we have people sell, and you name it, I've seen someone sell chiropractors, chiropractors, almost everything you can sell with this script. And this is literally 10 years of me standing in front of stage and embarrassing myself, and then learning something, trying something else, and going back and forth, and back and forth, and like, this is probably the best gift I could ever give anyone. And so a year and a half ago, someone named Liz Benny, it's me as many have heard of her, she joined our coaching program, she didn't have anything online happening yet, we gave her this script, she launched it, and then she followed the model I'll be talking about next, and first year, they're just shy of a million dollars, which is awesome. Jason O'Neill, another awesome guy in our coaching group, gave him the script, he took it, launched it, and his business, they are, I believe this month, they're gonna be, this month or next month, they'll be passing seven figures in sales, and I can tell you person after person, after person after person, this script is it. Now everyone's like, it's someone who takes the script and they try it, and then they're like, Russell, it didn't work for me. That's what I'm on Facebook today, like I did it and it didn't work. And the reality is, it always works. If it didn't work, it means you screwed it up. And typically, this is the part everyone screws up, is this right here. They go out there and they wanna teach as much stuff as they possibly can, and the reality is, the teaching is not how you get somebody to buy. That was one of my big ahas I had during my decade, a falling flat on my face for you guys. That's not what gets people to buy. What gets people to buy is understanding their belief patterns, and then structuring the content around their belief pattern, finding a belief pattern, breaking it and rebuilding it into the actual truth. If you screw this part up, nobody will buy it no matter how good your offer is back here. And so, this is the key. It takes a minute to understand about if you get that. It's awesome. A lot of people think that you do a webinar once, you make some money, and then you're like, now what? I had a friend one time said that all those internet marketers are so stupid. He says, you guys go out there and you build these amazing funnels and these amazing things, and you launch it, and you have a big product launch, make a bunch of money, and then you stop the next day, so you're working on your next funnel, your next launch, your next thing, right? And her name was Mary Ellen Tripp, she's a genius. She said, what I do for entrepreneurs like you guys, she's like, what you guys do is the equivalent of putting on a Broadway play. You put on this huge play, it's amazing. You get all these people coming in, it's a smashing success on opening night, and the next day you close it and you start writing a new play. And she says, what entrepreneurs have to become good at doing is taking that play from, let's say you didn't, Boyz the Idaho is a success, now you take it, and you take it on the road, you go to Chicago, you go to New York, and that's how you scale a company, right? And so this is the key understanding the model here. So this is how the webinar model works, okay? Sorry, webinar model. All right, so the way it works is you are doing a live webinar every single Thursday for the foreseeable future, so here's Thursday. You are doing a webinar, okay? This is a live, this is like the play, you've been preparing and writing the script, making this play amazing, so Thursday this play is happening live. Now Thursday this event's happening, you gotta get people to that event, right? So what happens is on Monday and on Tuesday, and on Wednesday, your entire goal and your focus of your entire company should be getting people to register for this event that's happening on Thursday, right? That's the whole focus. If you start earlier than that, if you start Sunday, the people will not remember who you are by Thursday, so we do not start ads until Monday. Monday, we start ads, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, push ads on Thursday webinar, Thursday webinar, we get on, we follow the perfect webinar script, we crush it, we make a bunch of money, and then Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, these three days, you focus on converting the people who missed the webinar, who didn't buy whatever, and getting these guys to buy here. And that is the webinar model, okay? And so Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, and Thursday, until you do the webinar, you're promoting it, Thursday you do the webinar, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, you're closing sales, doing everything, Sunday, and midnight this offer disappears for every single person, and ads start again on Monday for the next webinar after next Thursday. Oops, excuse me, and that, you guys, is the model. I'm not giving you guys the ability to say, oh, I'm gonna try once or twice, or you know, you've gotta do it consistently every single month. The way that we do it in our company right now, we have a goal where every single week, I want 1,000 people registered for this webinar. So Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, we're focusing on Facebook ads, solo ads, email ads, joint meds, everything possible, to get 1,000 people to register. And so I went back and changed my presentation. I added a bunch of slides to kind of address that concern. Got back home from the event and I did the very first live webinar. We made about $30,000 from the webinar. I'm like, dang it, that was good, but it wasn't awesome. And what was interesting about that is four hours later, I had four hours before my second webinar to sell the same thing. And so I had this four hour gap, finished the webinar. So what I did, I went to go to the webinar at the time, exported all the questions people asked me. I looked at them and I'm like, okay, people are confused here and they're stuck here. I looked at all the questions. Went back to my slides and started tweaking things as quick as I could, making everything right. Four hours later, I did the exact same webinar to a smaller audience.
